name: Egyptian mythology
shortDescription: Egyptian mythology encompasses the collection of myths, legends, and religious beliefs of ancient Egypt. It played a crucial role in shaping their culture, worldview, and understanding of the cosmos.
description: Egyptian mythology is a complex and rich tapestry of stories, beliefs, and rituals that formed the foundation of ancient Egyptian civilization. It provided explanations for the creation of the world, the nature of the gods, the afterlife, and the cycles of nature. Central to Egyptian mythology were the deities, each with their own distinct roles, powers, and associated symbols. These gods and goddesses were not merely abstract figures but active participants in the world, influencing events and interacting with humans. Myths served not only as entertainment but also as a means of conveying moral lessons, reinforcing social order, and ensuring the continuity of life. Key themes explored in Egyptian mythology include creation, resurrection, divine kingship, and the eternal struggle between order (Ma'at) and chaos (Isfet). The enduring influence of Egyptian mythology can be seen in its art, architecture, literature, and religious practices, leaving a lasting legacy on subsequent cultures and belief systems. The concept of the afterlife, with its elaborate rituals and beliefs surrounding mummification and judgment, is particularly prominent. The mythology is also deeply intertwined with the Nile River and the agricultural cycle, reflecting the Egyptians' dependence on the river for their survival.

sources.title: The Oxford Guide to Egyptian Mythology
sources.author: Donald B. Redford
